This one is friendly, well stocked, and keeps late hours so pay them a visit if you would like to pick up a copy of one of the many volumes of ''Lone Wolf and Cub'' in French. }} *{{buy | name=Mariage Frères | alt= | url= | email= | address=30 & 35 rue de Bourg-Tibourg | lat=48.85773 | long=2.35658 | directions=Métro: Hotel de Ville | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=If you love tea, you'll love this old salon de the in the Marais. }} *{{buy | name=BHV | alt=Bazar de l'Hôtel de Ville | url=http://www.bhv.fr | email= | address=52 rue de Rivoli | lat=48.85745 | long=2.353344 | directions=Metro Hôtel de Ville | phone=+33 1 42 74 90 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Bazar de l'Hôtel de Ville | wikidata=Q2892394 | content=Is a general store with most of everything: clothes, perfumes, furniture, you name it—it's especially famous for its basement section, dedicated to handiwork & DIY --think of it as a small ''Home Depot'' in a basement, though with French hardware and house and garden accessories that look distinctive in other settings. }} ==Eat== [[File:Tribunal de Commerce, Paris 140320 2.jpg|thumb|Tribunal de Commerce]] For dinner or a sit-down lunch there are over a thousand restaurants in the 4th catering to all tastes - many more deserve to be listed than this or any other guide has space for. There are nice places, trendy or traditional throughout the district, but most of the really fancy bistros are clustered around the NW corner of Place de Bastille. You'll really enjoy walking around and checking out the menus, especially during the week when only the most exclusive places require a reservation. That said, here are some ideas: ===Budget=== If you are looking for a snack or a quick lunch you could do a lot worse than any one of the kosher falafel stands along the rue des Rosiers near Place des Vosges. If you are on the île de la Cité though, a closer choice is just to hop across the little pedestrian bridge to the ''île Saint-Louis'' for lunch at any one of the many charming cafés. *{{eat | name=Chez Marianne | alt= | url= | email= | address=2, Rue des Hospitalières-Saint Gervais | lat=48.857762 | long=2.358579 | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 88 64 15 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Falafel out of the window is €4; in the dining room you'll pay around €15 for a vegetarian menu, or up to €22 for the most expensive dishes à la carte. | content=At the corner of rue des Hospitalières-Saint Gervais, and the rue Rosiers, Chez Marianne—like the many falafel stands in the ''Pletzle''—serves excellent sandwiches out of a walk up window, but unlike some of the others also has an attractive dining room and a truly lovely terrace where you can enjoy a full range of Jewish/middle-eastern dishes.}} *{{eat | name=La Perla | alt= | url=http://www.cafepacifico-laperla.com/ | email= | address=26 rue François Miron | lat=48.855795 | long=2.356132 | directions=Metro: Hôtel de Ville | phone=+33 1 42 77 59 40 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 11:00-14:00 | price=You'll pay about €10 for a plate ordered à la carte | content=Good Mexican food to be had within a stone's throw of the Ile de St. Louis. They mix a decent margarita too!}} * {{eat | name=Flunch | alt= | url=http://restaurant.flunch.fr/paris/flunch-paris-beaubourg/#_=_ | email=1172d@flunch.fr | address=21 Rue de Beaubourg | lat= | long= | directions=Metro Rambuteau | phone=+33 1 40290978 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=08:00-22:00 | price=Around €8 per person | lastedit=2016-07-05 | content=Self-service restaurant, right next to the Pompidou centre. Steaks etc. are cooked fresh when ordered, other food is pre-cooked and there is a microwave in the table area should you need to heat anything up. A chance to try authentic French food at cheap prices. Just one minus - there is a free water and ice dispender next to the microwave, in the table area, AFTER you've paid and probably bought another drink! Price - e.g. one couscous, one steak with mixed vegetables, two pieces of bread, €16.50. }} ===Mid-range=== *{{eat | name=O Corcovado Marais | alt= | url=http://www.ocorcovado.com/ | email=info@ocorcovado.com | address=7, rue Simon-Lefranc | lat=48.860026 | long=2.353783 | directions=Metro: Rambuteau | phone=+33 6 60 40 08 58 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 19:00- | price= | content=This Brazilian restaurant serves authentic food.}} *{{eat | name=Creperie Beaubourg | alt= | url=http://creperiebeaubourg.com/ | email= | address=2 Rue Brisemiche | lat=48.859125 | long=2.351513 | directions=Métro: Hotel de Ville | phone=+33 1 42 77 63 62 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=11:30-23:00 | price=€10 | content=The restaurant serves salty "galette" and sweet "crepes" with cider for a reasonable price. In summertime one can sit nicely outdoor.}} *{{eat | name=Le Loir dans la Théière | alt= | url= | email= | address=3, rue des Rosiers | lat=48.856236 | long=2.361044 | directions=Metro: Saint Paul | phone=+33 1 42 72 90 61 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00-19:30 | price= | content=A highly recommended and quaint little tea shop, well worth a visit especially for brunch, which at €15.50 is a great value for the quantity, quality, and ambience. The cakes are huge, but if one is too late they might already be gone.}} *{{eat | name=Pain Vin Fromages | alt= | url=http://www.painvinfromage.com/ | email= | address=3, rue Geoffrey l'Angevin | lat=48.860529 | long=2.354475 | directions=Métro Rambuteau | phone=+33 1 42 74 07 52 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=It's all about cheese, with a selection of Swiss dishes, and others.}} * {{eat | name=Le Colimaçon | alt= | url=https://www.le-colimacon.fr/en/ | email= | address=44 Rue Vieille du Temple | lat=48.85808008778574 | long=2.3580667026053796 | directions=Métro Hôtel de Ville | phone=+33 1 48 87 12 01 | tollfree= | hours=6:30PM - 10:45PM; Sunday Brunch from 12:30PM - 2:30PM | price=€62 | lastedit=2020-11-24 | content=Cozy, charming space. Attentive service. French cuisine with daily specials. }} ===Splurge=== * {{eat | name=L'Ambroisie | alt= | url=http://www.ambroisie-paris.com/ | email= | address=9, place des Vosges | lat=48.855396 | long=2.364372 | directions=Métro: Sain Paul | phone=+33 1 42 78 51 45 | tollfree= | hours=Tu-Sa 12:00-13:15, 20:00-22:00 | price=€500 | wikipedia=L'Ambroisie | wikidata=Q6455533 | lastedit=2020-03-01 | content=In the aristocratic décors of the 18th century the chef Bernard Pacaud and his son prepare modern and refined food. The "tarte au chocolat" is surreal. }} *{{eat | name=Brasserie Bofinger | alt= | url=http://www.bofingerparis.com/ | email= | address=3, rue de la Bastille | lat=48.853894 | long=2.36806 | directions=Metro: Bastille | phone=+33 1 42 72 87 82 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A beautiful, historical ''décor'' in the ''Art Nouveau'' style (see the ceiling upstairs); good Alsatian cuisine : ''choucroute'', kuglopf, etc, plus good classic ''cuisine de brasserie''. }} *{{eat | name=Le Georges | alt= | url=http://restaurantgeorgesparis.com/en/ | email= | address=19 rue Beaubourg | lat=48.860949 | long=2.352817 | directions= | phone=+33 1 44 78 47 99 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=M 11:00-24:00, W-Su 11:00-02:00 | content=On the top floor of the Centre Georges Pompidou, the restaurant Georges offers a panoramic view of Paris. Serves modern French food with classic desserts like "blancs en neige". }} ===Ice cream=== French ice cream is famous for its sorbet and there are excellent ice cream parlours to choose from: *{{eat | name=Amorino | alt= | url=http://amorino.fr | email=marais@amorino.fr | address=47, rue Saint Louis en l’Ile | lat=48.852048 | long=2.355752 | directions= | phone=+33 1 42 78 07 75 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The oldest of the many boutiques operated by this ''gelato'' (Italian ice cream) maker.}} *{{eat | name=Glacier Berthillon | alt= | url=http://www.berthillon.fr/ | email= | address=31 Rue Saint-Louis en l'Île | lat=48.851693 | long=2.356747 | directions=Métro: Pont Marie | phone=+33 1 43 54 31 61 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-Su 10:00-20:00 | price= | content=The most renowned French sorbet has his little tea shop since 1954. The wait in the queue is definitely worth it, also with some luck the queue isn't that big. On the corner, the queue for takeaway is usually bigger but, of course, faster. Taking the ice to the Seine is, of course. a possible option.}} *{{eat | name=Pozetto | alt= | url=http://www.pozzetto.biz/ | email= | address=39 Rue du Roi de Sicile | lat=48.856714 | long=2.356282 | directions=Métro: Hotel de Ville | phone=+33 1 42 77 08 64 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 12:15–23:45, F Sa 12:15-00:45, Su 12:15–23:45 | price= | content=Little shop that's both a café and sells Italian ice cream. The ice cream flavours vary according to the season. }} ==Drink== In the 4th it's really hard to say whether a given place is somewhere to '''Eat''' or somewhere to '''Drink'''. Most of the places on this list serve dinner, and some serve lunch as well. *{{drink | name=The Auld Alliance Scottish Pub | alt= | url=http://www.theauldalliance.com/ | email= | address=80, rue Francois Miron | lat= | long= | directions=Metro St Paul | phone=+33 1 48 04 30 40 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily, last orders 01:30 | price= | content=Friendly staff and customers help create a welcoming atmosphere at the original Scottish pub in Paris. Excellent food is served every day including a hearty brunch. The pub is selling quality British crisps, even smokey bacon flavour. A wide selection of whisky is on offer. Sporting events are shown on eight large television screens. Regular darts and pool competitions take place and Monday night is quiz night. }} *{{drink | name=Café des Phares | url= | email= | address=7, place de la Bastille | lat= | long= | directions=Métro: Bastille | phone=+33 1 4272 0470 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 07:00-03:00 | price= | content=Since 1992 this otherwise very attractive but fairly standard Parisian café has been host to a lively discussion of contemporary philosophy and attendant issues every Sunday night. There's a political discussion too, on the first Thursday of each month.}} *{{drink | name=Café Lutèce | url= | email= | address=33, quai de Bourbon | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Pont-Marie | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This little bistro would be totally unremarkable if it weren't for the location, on the north bank of '''Ile Saint-Louis''', where you can watch the colours of the sunset light play over the towers of the '''Hôtel de Ville''' as you enjoy a glass or three of beer or house wine, or maybe a cognac.}} *{{drink | name=Le Lizard Lounge | url= | email= | address=18, rue du Bourg-Tibourg | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Hôtel-de-Ville | phone=+33 1 42 72 48 34 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=}} *{{drink | name=Le Petit Fer A Cheval | url= | email= | address=30, rue Vieille-du Temple | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 42 72 47 47 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=}} *{{drink | name=La Chaise au Plafond | url= | email= | address=10, rue du Trésor | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 42 76 03 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The Chaise is one of those truly warm and welcoming cafés. The proprietor usually makes an effort to spend at least a couple of minutes at each table, and somehow manages to remember visitors even years later.}} *{{drink | name=Les Etages | url= | email= | address=35, rue Vieille du Temple | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 42 78 72 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=}} *{{drink | name=Stolly's | url= | email= | address=7, rue Cloche-Perce | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 42 76 06 76 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=}} *{{drink | name=Caféothèque de Paris | alt= | url=http://www.lacafeotheque.com/ | email= | address=52, rue de l’Hôtel de Ville | lat=48.854448 | long=2.355762 | directions=Metro: Pont Marie | phone=+33 1 52 01 83 84 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 09:30-19:30 | price= | content=Coffee in Paris is usually found to be lacking, but not in this mini temple to coffee. A gem tucked into a backstreet by the river in le Marais roasts their own beans from around the world and has a small laid-back café area with armchairs. Staff are passionate about great coffee, not just a scaldingly hot coffee! They have some cakes and wine, and speak Spanish and English.}} *{{drink | name=Dome Du Marais | url=http://www.ledomedumarais.fr | email= | address=53 bis, rue De Francs Bourgeois | lat= | long= | directions= | phone = +33 1 42 74 54 17 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=}} *{{drink | name=Wake Up Paris | url= | email= | address=31, boul Henri IV | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Bastille | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A wonderful little bar with a Brazilian tilt that serves excellent caipirinhas. The staff is friendly and eager to strike up a conversation, especially about cachaça, the Brazilian liqueur that forms the base of their signature drink. It's a wonderful spot for catching a football match as well.}} * {{drink | name=Queen Ann | alt= | url= | email= | address=5 rue Simon le Franc | lat=48.860023 | long=2.353834 | directions=Métro: Rambuteau | phone=+33 1 42 78 00 07 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 12:00-19:00 | price=€22-25 for a brunch | content=The "salon de thé" serves soufflés and cakes. The small place has 12 tables. The hot chocolate makes the place highly addictable. }} ==Sleep== There are tons of hotels, hostels, furnished apartments and guest houses in the 4th. Even still you should book ahead if possible as there is also tons of demand. ===Budget=== * {{sleep | name=MIJE | url=http://www.mije.com | email= | address=6, rue de Fourcy, 12 rue des Barres, and 11 rue du Fauconnier | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: St. Paul | phone=+33 1 42 74 23 45 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Dorm-room beds start at €32 at all three places. Single rooms are €52 | checkin= | checkout= | content=Consistently given the highest possible ratings this organization actually runs three hostels in the Marais. The only possible drawback is that they have an afternoon lockout for cleaning. The MIJE Maubuisson on rue des Barres gets the nod of the three for being in an historic Tudor-style building. }} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel Rivoli | url= | email= | address=44, rue Rivoli | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Hotel de Ville | phone=+33 1 42 72 08 41 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=€30+ | checkin= | checkout= | content=In the heart of the city with cheap doubles. Terrific view from 5th floor single room.}} *{{sleep | name=Grand Hôtel du Loiret | url= | email= | address=8, rue des Mauvais Garçons | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Hôtel-de-Ville | phone=+33 1 48 87 77 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=€45, or €60 with a shower | checkin= | checkout= | content=A good value for the price and location.}} *{{sleep | name=Grand Hôtel Jeanne-d'Arc | url=http://www.hoteljeannedarc.com | email= | address=3, rue de Jarente | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 48 87 62 11 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=€57 for a basic room | checkin= | checkout= | content=In the calmer part of the Marais, not far from Place de Bastille and Place des Vosges, this little hotel fills up well in advance, so you'll need to book ahead.}} ===Mid-range=== *{{sleep | name=Hôtel du Septième Art | alt= | url= | email= | address=20 rue Saint-Paul | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul | phone=+33 1 44 54 85 00 | tollfree= | fax=+33 1 42 77 69 10 | checkin= | checkout= | price=€75+ | content=A little place done up in all black and white in homage to the cinema, the 7th Art offers a good value on comfortable rooms, and polite, quality service. Single rooms include air conditioning, WC, and shower. A 5-min promenade to the Rue de Rivoli.}} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel Hospitel | url=http://www.hotel-hospitel.com {{dead link|August 2018|December 2020}} | email= | address=1, Place du Parvis Notre Dame-Galerie B2, 6F | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Cité | phone=+33 1 44 32 01 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Single €88.50, double €99.50 | checkin= | checkout= | content=You can't beat the location of this no-star but mid-range offering on the ''Ile de la Cité'' located on the 6th floor of the ''Hospital Hôtel-Dieu'' a functioning hospital which is also classified as an official historical monument. '''Hôspitel''' offers quite a bit of service and comfort.}} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel Bastille Speria | url=http://www.hotel-bastille-speria.com | email= | address=1, rue de la Bastille | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Bastille | phone=+33 1 42 72 04 01 | tollfree= | fax=+33 1 42 72 56 38 | hours= | price=€95 for a single | checkin= | checkout= | content=This lovely little three-star is at the far end of the 4th bumped right up against Place de Bastille. The place is inviting and the rooms are quite comfy and air conditioned. The location is good especially if you are interested in fine dining, as the place is surrounded by some of the better bistros on the right bank.}} *{{sleep | name=Hotel Caron de Beaumarchais | url=http://www.carondebeaumarchais.com/ | email= | address=12, rue Vieille-du-Temple | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Saint-Paul or Hôtel-de-Ville | phone=+33 1 42 72 34 12 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Single for €137 | checkin= | checkout= | content=These are of the expected (small) size for Paris though elegantly appointed in 18th-century style. Equipment includes air conditioning. Tea and coffee at the reception. The courtyard is a two-by-two metre light shaft, so rooms facing it have no view, and are quite dark on the ground and first floors. A simple request to avoid them on booking will suffice. The staff speak English well.}} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel de Lutece | url=http://www.paris-hotel-lutece.com | email= | address=65, rue Saint-Louis-en-l'Ile | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Pont-Marie | phone=+33 1 43 26 23 52 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=€158 | checkin= | checkout= | content=}} *{{sleep | name=Hotel Notre Dame | url=http://hotel-paris-notredame.com | email= | address=19, rue Maître Alberte | lat= | long= | directions= | phone = +33 9 77 19 84 17 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | checkin= | checkout= | content=On a typical and quiet little street, looking out on the Seine and facing Notre Dame Cathedral. Every room is uniquely styled and decorated and equipped with a complete, marble bathroom.}} *{{sleep | name=Jardins de Paris Marais-Bastille | url=http://www.hotelsjardinsdeparis.com | email= | address=14, rue Neuve-Saint-Pierre | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Bastille | phone=+33 1 44 59 28 50 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Singles for €120, doubles for €135 | checkin= | checkout= | content=}} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel Central | url= | email= | address=5, rue saint Paul | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Hôtel de Ville | phone=+33 6 71 24 76 98 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | checkin= | checkout= | content=}} * {{sleep | name=Hôtel de la Place des Vosges | url=http://hotelplacedesvosges.com/ | email= | address=12 rue de Birague | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Bastille or Saint-Paul | phone=+33 9 77 21 78 62 | tollfree= | fax=+33 1 42 72 02 64 | hours= | price=€101-140 | checkin= | checkout= | content=The rooms are tiny, as is the staircase, but they are also pristine, some of the rooms have shiny marble bathrooms. The staff also gets high marks for professionalism and for general helpfulness.}} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel de Nice | url=http://www.hoteldenice.com/ | email= | address=42bis, rue de Rivoli | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Hôtel de Ville | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=€60+ | checkin= | checkout= | content=Just two blocks from the Ile de St. Louis, the Hôtel de Nice offers tiny but lovely rooms. Ask for one with a balcony, some of which have a view of Notre Dame. The furniture is hand-painted with a floral theme which continues throughout the room. The area can be a little loud though, so if you want peace and quiet look elsewhere.}} *{{sleep | name=Hôtel Rivoli | url= | email= | address=44 rue de Rivoli | lat= | long= | directions=Metro: Hôtel de Ville | phone=+33 1 42 72 08 41 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Double for €48 | checkin= | checkout= | content=Great location. Can be noisy if you have a room which faces the street.}} * {{sleep | name=Hôtel Duo | alt= | url=https://duo-paris.com | email=contact@duoparis.com | address=11 Rue du Temple | lat=48.858211381999766 | long=2.3529062463729713 | directions=in the Marais | phone=+33 1 42 72 72 22 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price= | lastedit=2020-11-24 | content=Stylish, modern and very clean boutique hotel. }} ==Connect== *{{listing | name=Bibliothèque Publique d'Information | url= | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This library in Centre Pompidou offers free Internet for 40 min.
